name: Regional Park of Serra Espuña — Circular via the Leiva Ravine (PR-MU 57)
name_original: Parque Regional de Serra Espuña — Circular por el Barranco de Leiva (PR-MU 57)
region_intro: Easy hiking trails around National Park Sierra Espuña traverse a diverse landscape in Spain's Murcia region. The park features extensive pine forests, Mediterranean woodland, and unique arid terrains like the Barrancos de Gebas, often described as a "lunar landscape." Its topography includes dramatic cliffs, plateaus, and mountain peaks, with elevations reaching over 1,500 meters. Rivers and reservoirs also contribute to the park's varied natural environment. Best easy hiking trails around National Park Sierra Espuña The most popular easy hiking route is Hiking loop from Cabezo de los Castillicos, a 2.7 miles (4.4 km) trail that takes 1 hour 20 minutes to complete, offering gentle gradients through natural surroundings. Another top favourite among local hikers is Collado Bermejo – Peña Apartada loop from Parque Regional de Sierra Espuña, an easy 3.6 miles (5.8 km) path. This route provides views of the park's varied terrain, including forested areas and open spaces. Local hikers also love the Hiking loop from La Perdiz, a 2.0 miles (3.2 km) trail leading through woodland and open areas, often completed in about 51 minutes. Hiking around National Park Sierra Espuña is defined by extensive pine forests, unique lunar landscapes of the Barrancos de Gebas, and varied mountain peaks. The network offers options for different ability levels, including many easy hiking trails. The routes in National Park Sierra Espuña are highly rated by the komoot community with an average score of 4.6 stars from more than 370 reviews. More than 1800 hikers have used komoot to explore National Park Sierra Espuña's varied terrain.
region_faq:
Q: How many easy hiking trails are available in National Park Sierra Espuña?
A: National Park Sierra Espuña offers over 20 easy hiking trails, making it an ideal destination for relaxed walks amidst diverse landscapes. In total, there are more than 100 hiking routes across all difficulty levels.

Q: What kind of landscapes can I expect on easy hikes in Sierra Espuña?
A: Easy hikes in Sierra Espuña traverse a variety of landscapes, from extensive pine forests and Mediterranean woodlands to unique arid terrains like the Barrancos de Gebas, often described as a 'lunar landscape.' You'll also encounter dramatic cliffs, plateaus, and views of distant mountain peaks.

Q: Are there any circular easy hiking routes in National Park Sierra Espuña?
A: Yes, many easy routes in Sierra Espuña are circular. For example, the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/14626181" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Hiking loop from Cabezo de los Castillicos&lt;/a&gt; offers gentle gradients through natural surroundings, and the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/38244243" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Hiking loop from La Perdiz&lt;/a&gt; leads through woodland and open areas.

Q: What are some interesting natural features or landmarks to see on easy trails?
A: While exploring easy trails, you can discover several notable features. The unique 'lunar landscapes' of the Barrancos de Gebas are a highlight, offering stunning geological formations. You might also pass by the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/2953991" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Collado Bermejo&lt;/a&gt; mountain pass or enjoy panoramic views from the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/4840241" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Airplane Viewpoint&lt;/a&gt;.

Q: Are the easy hiking trails in Sierra Espuña suitable for families with children?
A: Yes, many easy trails in National Park Sierra Espuña are well-suited for families. They typically feature gentle gradients and shorter distances, allowing children to enjoy the natural surroundings comfortably. The park's diverse flora and fauna also provide engaging points of interest for younger hikers.

Q: Can I bring my dog on the easy hiking trails in National Park Sierra Espuña?
A: Generally, dogs are permitted on trails in National Park Sierra Espuña, but it's always recommended to keep them on a leash to protect the local wildlife and ensure a pleasant experience for all hikers. Please check specific trail regulations if you plan to visit sensitive areas.

Q: What is the best time of year to go for an easy hike in Sierra Espuña?
A: The best time for easy hikes in Sierra Espuña is typically during the spring and autumn months when temperatures are milder and the natural scenery is vibrant. Summer can be very hot, especially at lower altitudes, while winter offers cooler, crisp air, though higher elevations may experience colder conditions.

Q: Where can I find information or assistance before starting a hike?
A: The &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/704353" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Ricardo Codorníu Visitor Center&lt;/a&gt; is an excellent resource for information, maps, and details about guided routes. It's advisable to visit before your hike to get the latest updates on trail conditions and any specific park regulations.

Q: What do other hikers say about the easy trails in National Park Sierra Espuña?
A: The easy trails in National Park Sierra Espuña are highly rated by the komoot community, with an average score of 4.6 stars from over 370 reviews. Hikers often praise the well-maintained paths, the tranquility of the pine forests, and the unique geological formations like the Barrancos de Gebas.

Q: Are there any historical sites accessible via easy hiking routes?
A: While not directly on every easy route, the park is known for its historical 'Snow Pits' (Pozos de Nieve), 16th-century structures used to store snow for ice production. These are located at higher altitudes, but some easy trails might offer views or lead to areas with historical significance, such as the vicinity of the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/705186" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Hermitage of Santa Eulalia&lt;/a&gt;.

Q: What is the typical duration for an easy hike in Sierra Espuña?
A: The duration of easy hikes varies, but many can be completed within 1 to 2 hours. For instance, the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/38244051" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Peña Apartada loop from Parque Regional de Sierra Espuña&lt;/a&gt; takes about 1 hour and 5 minutes, while the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/27628286" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;La Perdiz Sanatorium Trail loop from La Perdiz&lt;/a&gt; is around 1 hour and 20 minutes.
tips: The route runs through the Sierra Espuña Regional Park and ascends to the Mangueta pass, Morrón de Alhama and the Morra de las Moscas peak.

Another of the peaks of Sierra Espuña with 1444 meters above sea level and with spectacular views of the entire regional park and the beautiful landscapes of the Guadalentín valley.
Remember to wear appropriate footwear because you will have to do some rock climbing to reach the top. It is not difficult, but it does require caution and a firm step.
